contraventionDescription: Failing to comply with a give way to oncoming vehicles sign. Contravention Code 37

sections.heading: Failing to comply with a give way to oncoming vehicles sign. Contravention Code 37
sections.content: Local authorities are now enforcing this contravention using CCTV
sections.contentHtml: Local authorities are now enforcing this contravention using CCTV

sections.heading: Sample signs:
sections.contentHtml: 37 Give way to oncoming traffic.

sections.heading: How to have your penalty charge notice (PCN) for this parking contravention cancelled.
sections.content: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.
sections.contentHtml: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.

sections.heading: Follow our 3 stage process:
sections.content:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
2 Check: What a Penalty Charge Notice must contain
3 Check: Statutory grounds for appeal for Moving Traffic Contraventions
sections.contentHtml:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)

2 Check: What a Penalty Charge Notice must contain

3 Check: Statutory grounds for appeal for Moving Traffic Contraventions

sections.heading: Specific grounds of appeal for this contravention
sections.content:
As the contravention is that of “failing to comply with a give way to oncoming vehicles sign it is important to remember that in order to prove this contravention that the enforcing authority must actually show you passing the sign at the exact time when an oncoming vehicle is approaching. It is not sufficient to simply show your vehicle. You can ask to see the video or photographic evidence. Even if they have changed the wording of the contravention to exclude the word sign the fact is that there still must be a sign there and if there is not then it is not enforceable.
There are 8 more ways that you can have your penalty charge notice cancelled for this offence. We have even summarised adjudicator’s decisions where motorists have won.
Was the signage clear and placed before the area where you should give way to oncoming vehicles.
Were there road markings, if so did they contain exactly the same restrictions as the signs. Appeals have been upheld where wording on the road did not exactly match the sign.
If there are restrictions at certain times were times stated on the Penalty charge notice (PCN) actually the same as on the signs.
The PCN must also show a picture of the sign which is essential if the contravention is “failing to comply with a sign” etc This only applies if the contravention is failing to comply with a sign however in any event the signage must be clear and match the diagrams.
The give way to oncoming vehicle signs must be placed on at least one side of the road and be clearly visible otherwise the contravention is not enforceable.
Are the signs the correct size (see diagrams above)
Were there any other signs or road markings that contradict the restriction?
Also were there signs warning of CCTV camera enforcement in the vicinity. Whilst these are not compulsory under the traffic management act they are compulsory under the guidelines issued by the information commissioner’s office (ICO) There is no guarantee however that an adjudicator would rule in your favour as we had a case where he did not. although in another case the adjudicator did!
Also do not forget that you can cite compassionate grounds.
Now take a look at the adjudicators decisions for this contravention.
Back to Moving Traffic
